1.title: Lloyd Mangrum born in Trenton, Texas
1.text: Lloyd Mangrum, the 1946 U.S. Open champion and winner of 36 PGA Tour titles, was born on August 1, 1914. He earned two Purple Hearts in World War II before returning to dominate post-war golf.

1.title: Putting wizard Brad Faxon born
1.text: American Brad Faxon, an eight-time PGA Tour winner and two-time Ryder Cup player renowned as one of the finest putters of his era, was born on August 1, 1961.

1.title: Al Geiberger wins Western Open
1.text: On August 1, 1976, Al Geiberger won the Western Open, one of his 11 career PGA Tour titles and a prelude to his historic 59 the following year.

1.title: Nick Price wins Western Open at Cog Hill
1.text: On August 1, 1993, Nick Price won the Western Open at Cog Hill, part of a dominant stretch that would carry him to back-to-back PGA Championships.

1.title: Stewart Cink wins WGC-NEC Invitational
1.text: On August 1, 2004, Stewart Cink won the WGC-NEC Invitational at Firestone, holding off Rory Sabbatini and Tiger Woods by four for one of his biggest titles.

1.title: Hunter Mahan wins WGC-Bridgestone Invitational
1.text: On August 1, 2010, Hunter Mahan won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone, holding off Ryan Palmer for the most significant title of his career.

2.title: Four-time Open champion Bobby Locke born
2.text: South African Bobby Locke, a four-time Open Championship winner celebrated for his masterful putting, was born on August 2, 1924, in Germiston.

2.title: PGA champion Don January born
2.text: American Don January, the 1967 PGA Championship winner and one of the dominant figures of the early senior tour, was born on August 2, 1932, in Plainview, Texas.

2.title: Ben Crenshaw wins Western Open
2.text: On August 2, 1992, Ben Crenshaw won the Western Open at Cog Hill, one of the 19 PGA Tour titles of his Hall of Fame career.

2.title: Tiger Woods wins WGC-Bridgestone Invitational
2.text: On August 2, 2009, Tiger Woods won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone for his seventh title there, beating Robert Allenby and Padraig Harrington by four.

2.title: Shane Lowry wins WGC-Bridgestone Invitational
2.text: On August 2, 2015, Shane Lowry won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone for his first PGA Tour victory, holding off Bubba Watson and Justin Rose.

3.title: Hale Irwin wins Western Open
3.text: On August 3, 1975, Hale Irwin won the Western Open at Butler National, one of the many titles collected by the three-time U.S. Open champion.

3.title: Bob Tway wins Western Open
3.text: On August 3, 1986, Bob Tway won the Western Open, days before his famous holed bunker shot would win him the PGA Championship at Inverness.

3.title: Tiger Woods wins Western Open
3.text: On August 3, 1997, Tiger Woods won the Motorola Western Open at Cog Hill, an early title in the historic 1997 season that began with his record Masters win.

3.title: Tiger Woods wins WGC-NEC Invitational
3.text: On August 3, 2003, Tiger Woods won the WGC-NEC Invitational at Firestone, adding to his dominant record on the Akron course.

3.title: Vijay Singh wins WGC-Bridgestone Invitational
3.text: On August 3, 2008, Vijay Singh won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone, beginning a late-season run that would carry him to the FedEx Cup title.

3.title: Rory McIlroy wins WGC-Bridgestone Invitational
3.text: On August 3, 2014, Rory McIlroy won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone, his third win in a row before he captured the PGA Championship a week later.

4.title: 'Lighthorse' Harry Cooper born
4.text: English-American Harry Cooper, who won 31 PGA Tour events yet never a major, was born on August 4, 1904. He was nicknamed 'Lighthorse' for his rapid pace of play.

4.title: Jack Nicklaus wins American Golf Classic
4.text: On August 4, 1968, Jack Nicklaus won the American Golf Classic at Firestone, one of many victories he recorded on the Akron course.

4.title: Tom Watson wins Western Open for first Tour title
4.text: On August 4, 1974, Tom Watson won the Western Open at Butler National for his first PGA Tour victory, launching a Hall of Fame career.

4.title: Scott Verplank wins Western Open as an amateur
4.text: On August 4, 1985, amateur Scott Verplank won the Western Open in a playoff, becoming the first amateur to win a PGA Tour event since 1954.

4.title: Craig Parry wins WGC-NEC Invitational
4.text: On August 4, 2002, Australian Craig Parry won the WGC-NEC Invitational at Sahalee, beating Robert Allenby in a playoff for the biggest win of his career.

4.title: Tiger Woods wins eighth WGC-Bridgestone title
4.text: On August 4, 2013, Tiger Woods won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone for the eighth time, aided by a second-round 61, by seven strokes.

5.title: Patrick Reed born in San Antonio, Texas
5.text: Patrick Reed, the 2018 Masters champion and a fiery Ryder Cup competitor known as 'Captain America,' was born on August 5, 1990.

5.title: Bruce Crampton wins American Golf Classic
5.text: On August 5, 1973, Australian Bruce Crampton won the American Golf Classic at Firestone during one of his strongest PGA Tour seasons.

5.title: Wayne Levi wins Western Open
5.text: On August 5, 1990, Wayne Levi won the Western Open at Butler National, one of four titles in a career year that earned him PGA Tour Player of the Year.

5.title: Tiger Woods wins third straight WGC-NEC Invitational
5.text: On August 5, 2001, Tiger Woods beat Jim Furyk in a playoff to win the WGC-NEC Invitational at Firestone for the third year in a row.

5.title: Tiger Woods wins WGC-Bridgestone Invitational
5.text: On August 5, 2007, Tiger Woods won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone, completing a three-peat from 2005 to 2007 just ahead of his PGA Championship win.

5.title: Keegan Bradley wins WGC-Bridgestone Invitational
5.text: On August 5, 2012, Keegan Bradley won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone, beating Jim Furyk and Steve Stricker by one stroke.

6.title: Triumvirate member James Braid born
6.text: Scotland's James Braid, a five-time Open Championship winner and later a celebrated course architect, was born on August 6, 1870, in Earlsferry, Fife.

6.title: Two-time major winner Doug Ford born
6.text: American Doug Ford, the 1955 PGA Championship and 1957 Masters winner, was born on August 6, 1922. He was inducted into the World Golf Hall of Fame in 2011.

6.title: David Graham wins PGA Championship at Oakland Hills
6.text: On August 6, 1979, David Graham beat Ben Crenshaw in a sudden-death playoff to win the PGA Championship at Oakland Hills, the first of his two major titles.

6.title: Billy Mayfair wins Western Open
6.text: On August 6, 1995, Billy Mayfair won the Motorola Western Open at Cog Hill, one of his PGA Tour titles.

6.title: Tiger Woods wins WGC-NEC Invitational by 11
6.text: On August 6, 2000, Tiger Woods won the WGC-NEC Invitational at Firestone by 11 strokes, finishing the final holes in near-darkness during his historic 2000 season.

6.title: Hideki Matsuyama wins WGC-Bridgestone Invitational
6.text: On August 6, 2017, Hideki Matsuyama won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone by five strokes for one of the biggest titles of his career.

7.title: Sam Snead wins PGA Championship at Hermitage
7.text: In the summer of 1949 Sam Snead added the PGA Championship to the Masters title he had won that spring, a high point of his Hall of Fame career.

7.title: Seve Ballesteros stars in major summer
7.text: In August 1988 Spain's Seve Ballesteros was at his peak, having won the Open Championship at Royal Lytham weeks earlier with his trademark flair.

7.title: Nick Price wins PGA Championship at Southern Hills
7.text: On August 7, 1994, Nick Price won the PGA Championship at Southern Hills by six strokes, completing a season in which he won both the Open and the PGA.

7.title: Tiger Woods wins WGC-NEC Invitational in playoff
7.text: On August 7, 2005, Tiger Woods beat Chris DiMarco in a playoff to win the WGC-NEC Invitational at Firestone, the first of three straight wins there.

7.title: Dustin Johnson wins WGC-Bridgestone Invitational
7.text: On August 7, 2016, Dustin Johnson won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at Firestone, part of a dominant summer that followed his maiden major at the U.S. Open.

8.title: Webb Simpson born in Raleigh, North Carolina
8.text: Webb Simpson, the 2012 U.S. Open champion and 2018 Players Championship winner, was born on August 8, 1985.

8.title: Tiger Woods wins inaugural WGC-NEC Invitational
8.text: On August 8, 1999, Tiger Woods won the inaugural WGC-NEC Invitational at Firestone by a stroke over Phil Mickelson, days before his first PGA Championship.

8.title: Australian golfer Jarrod Lyle dies at 36
8.text: Australian PGA Tour player Jarrod Lyle, beloved for his courage through years of battling leukemia, died on August 8, 2018, at age 36.

8.title: Patrick Cantlay wins BMW Championship over DeChambeau
8.text: On August 8, 2021, Patrick Cantlay outlasted Bryson DeChambeau in a six-hole playoff to win the BMW Championship, a duel that earned him the 'Patty Ice' nickname.

8.title: Hall of Famer Chi Chi Rodriguez dies at 88
8.text: Juan 'Chi Chi' Rodriguez, the showman who won eight PGA Tour titles and 22 senior events, died on August 8, 2024, at age 88. He was a World Golf Hall of Fame member.

9.title: Bob Rosburg wins PGA Championship
9.text: On August 2, 1959 Bob Rosburg rallied late to win the PGA Championship at Minneapolis Golf Club, the only major title of his career.

9.title: Larry Nelson wins first PGA Championship at Atlanta
9.text: On August 9, 1981, Larry Nelson won the PGA Championship at Atlanta Athletic Club by four strokes over Fuzzy Zoeller for his first major title.

9.title: Larry Nelson wins second PGA at PGA National
9.text: On August 9, 1987, Larry Nelson beat Lanny Wadkins in a playoff at PGA National to win the PGA Championship for his second PGA title and third major.

9.title: Nick Price wins first major at Bellerive
9.text: On August 9, 1992, Nick Price won the PGA Championship at Bellerive for his first major title after years of agonizing near-misses.

9.title: Vijay Singh wins first major at Sahalee
9.text: On August 9, 1998, Vijay Singh won the PGA Championship at Sahalee, holding off Steve Stricker by two for the first of his three major titles.

10.title: Kenny Perry born in Elizabethtown, Kentucky
10.text: Kenny Perry, a 14-time PGA Tour winner and prolific Champions Tour player, was born on August 10, 1960. He lost the 2009 Masters in a playoff to Angel Cabrera.

10.title: Jack Nicklaus wins PGA Championship at Firestone
10.text: On August 10, 1975, Jack Nicklaus won the PGA Championship at Firestone by two strokes, the fourth of his record-tying five PGA titles.

10.title: Jack Nicklaus wins PGA at Oak Hill by seven
10.text: On August 10, 1980, Jack Nicklaus won the PGA Championship at Oak Hill by seven strokes, his fifth PGA title in the same year he won the U.S. Open.

10.title: Bob Tway holes bunker shot to win PGA Championship
10.text: On August 10-11, 1986, Bob Tway holed a greenside bunker shot on the 72nd hole at Inverness to beat Greg Norman and win the PGA Championship, his only major.

10.title: Padraig Harrington wins PGA at Oakland Hills
10.text: On August 10, 2008, Padraig Harrington won the PGA Championship at Oakland Hills by two over Ben Curtis and Sergio Garcia, his second major in three weeks.

10.title: Rory McIlroy wins PGA Championship at Valhalla
10.text: On August 10, 2014, Rory McIlroy won the PGA Championship at Valhalla by one over Phil Mickelson, his second PGA title and fourth major.

11.title: Hubert Green wins PGA Championship at Cherry Hills
11.text: On August 11, 1985, Hubert Green won the PGA Championship at Cherry Hills, holding off Lee Trevino by two for the second major title of his career.

11.title: John Daly wins PGA Championship at Crooked Stick
11.text: On August 11, 1991, ninth alternate John Daly stunned golf by winning the PGA Championship at Crooked Stick by three strokes in his major debut.

11.title: Mark Brooks wins PGA Championship at Valhalla
11.text: On August 11, 1996, Mark Brooks beat Kenny Perry in a playoff to win the PGA Championship at Valhalla for his only major title.

11.title: Jason Dufner wins PGA Championship at Oak Hill
11.text: On August 11, 2013, Jason Dufner shot a final-round 68 to win the PGA Championship at Oak Hill by two over Jim Furyk for his first major title.

11.title: Patrick Reed wins Northern Trust playoff opener
11.text: On August 11, 2019, Patrick Reed won The Northern Trust at Liberty National, the opening event of the FedEx Cup playoffs, by one stroke.

12.title: U.S. Open pioneer John McDermott born
12.text: John McDermott, the first American-born U.S. Open champion (1911) and still the youngest at age 19, was born on August 12, 1891, in Philadelphia.

12.title: Gary Player wins PGA Championship at Aronimink
12.text: On July 22, 1962 Gary Player won the PGA Championship at Aronimink; he remained a global force across five decades of major golf.

12.title: Lee Trevino wins PGA Championship at Shoal Creek
12.text: On August 12, 1984, Lee Trevino won the PGA Championship at Shoal Creek by four strokes, the sixth and final major title of his Hall of Fame career.

12.title: Wayne Grady wins PGA Championship at Shoal Creek
12.text: On August 12, 1990, Australian Wayne Grady won the PGA Championship at Shoal Creek by three strokes for his only major title.

12.title: Rory McIlroy wins PGA Championship at Kiawah by eight
12.text: On August 12, 2012, Rory McIlroy won the PGA Championship at Kiawah Island by eight strokes, a record margin for the event and his second major.

12.title: Brooks Koepka wins PGA Championship at Bellerive
12.text: On August 12, 2018, Brooks Koepka won the PGA Championship at Bellerive over Tiger Woods, the last August edition before the major moved to May.

13.title: Ben Hogan born in Stephenville, Texas
13.text: Ben Hogan, winner of nine major championships and one of only six men to complete the career Grand Slam, was born on August 13, 1912.

13.title: John Mahaffey wins PGA Championship at Oakmont
13.text: On August 6, 1978 John Mahaffey came from seven back to beat Jerry Pate and Tom Watson in a playoff at Oakmont for his only major title.

13.title: Payne Stewart wins PGA Championship at Kemper Lakes
13.text: On August 13, 1989, Payne Stewart rallied past Mike Reid to win the PGA Championship at Kemper Lakes for the first major title of his career.

13.title: Steve Elkington wins PGA Championship at Riviera
13.text: On August 13, 1995, Steve Elkington sank a long birdie putt to beat Colin Montgomerie in a playoff at Riviera and win the PGA Championship, his only major.

13.title: Tiger Woods wins PGA Championship at Medinah
13.text: On August 20, 2006 Tiger Woods won the PGA Championship at Medinah by five strokes; in 2017 the major's August 13 finish would go to Justin Thomas.

13.title: Justin Thomas wins first major at Quail Hollow
13.text: On August 13, 2017, Justin Thomas won the PGA Championship at Quail Hollow for his first major title, capping a breakthrough season.

14.title: Open champion Darren Clarke born
14.text: Northern Ireland's Darren Clarke, the 2011 Open Championship winner and 2016 European Ryder Cup captain, was born on August 14, 1968, in Dungannon.

14.title: Lanny Wadkins wins PGA in first sudden-death playoff
14.text: On August 14, 1977, Lanny Wadkins beat Gene Littler in the first sudden-death playoff in major championship history to win the PGA at Pebble Beach.

14.title: Hal Sutton wins PGA Championship at Riviera
14.text: On August 14, 1983, Hal Sutton held off a charging Jack Nicklaus by one to win the PGA Championship at Riviera for his only major title.

14.title: Jeff Sluman wins PGA Championship at Oak Tree
14.text: On August 14, 1988, Jeff Sluman shot a closing 65 to win the PGA Championship at Oak Tree by three strokes for the only major of his career.

14.title: Keegan Bradley wins PGA Championship in playoff
14.text: On August 14, 2011, Keegan Bradley beat Jason Dufner in a playoff to win the PGA Championship at Atlanta Athletic Club in his major debut.

14.title: Jimmy Walker wins PGA Championship at Baltusrol
14.text: On July 31, 2016 Jimmy Walker won a rain-delayed PGA Championship at Baltusrol over Jason Day for his first major; in 2011 the August 14 final went to Keegan Bradley.

15.title: Gary Player rallies to win in major summer
15.text: By August 1965 Gary Player had completed the career Grand Slam with his U.S. Open win that June, joining an exclusive club of golf's greatest champions.

15.title: Paul Azinger wins PGA Championship at Inverness
15.text: On August 15, 1993, Paul Azinger beat Greg Norman in a sudden-death playoff at Inverness to win the PGA Championship for his only major title.

15.title: Tiger Woods wins first PGA Championship at Medinah
15.text: On August 15, 1999, Tiger Woods held off 19-year-old Sergio Garcia by one at Medinah to win his first PGA Championship and second major.

15.title: Vijay Singh wins PGA at Whistling Straits
15.text: On August 15, 2004, Vijay Singh won the PGA Championship at Whistling Straits in a three-hole playoff over Justin Leonard and Chris DiMarco for his third major.

15.title: Martin Kaymer wins PGA Championship at Whistling Straits
15.text: On August 15, 2010, Martin Kaymer beat Bubba Watson in a playoff to win the PGA Championship at Whistling Straits, in a finish marred by Dustin Johnson's bunker penalty.

15.title: Patrick Cantlay wins BMW Championship era milestone
15.text: In mid-August 2021 the FedEx Cup playoffs reached their height, with Patrick Cantlay's BMW Championship marathon playoff win positioning him for the season title.

16.title: Dave Stockton wins first PGA Championship
16.text: On August 16, 1970, Dave Stockton won the PGA Championship at Southern Hills by two strokes over Arnold Palmer and Bob Murphy for the first of his two PGA titles.

16.title: Larry Nelson wins first PGA at Atlanta Athletic Club
16.text: On August 9, 1981 Larry Nelson won the PGA Championship at Atlanta Athletic Club by four over Fuzzy Zoeller; he would add a second PGA and a U.S. Open to his major haul.

16.title: Nick Price wins first major at Bellerive
16.text: On August 16, 1992, Nick Price won the PGA Championship at Bellerive for the first of his three major titles after years of near-misses.

16.title: Vijay Singh wins PGA Championship at Sahalee
16.text: On August 16, 1998, Vijay Singh won the PGA Championship at Sahalee, holding off Steve Stricker by two for the first of his three majors.

16.title: Y.E. Yang beats Tiger Woods to win PGA at Hazeltine
16.text: On August 16, 2009, Y.E. Yang overtook a 54-hole-leading Tiger Woods to win the PGA Championship at Hazeltine, becoming the first Asian-born man to win a major.

16.title: Jason Day wins PGA with record 20-under at Whistling Straits
16.text: On August 16, 2015, Jason Day won the PGA Championship at Whistling Straits at 20-under par, the lowest score relative to par in major championship history.

17.title: Raymond Floyd wins first PGA Championship
17.text: On August 17, 1969, Raymond Floyd won the PGA Championship at NCR Country Club in Dayton for the first major title of his Hall of Fame career.

17.title: Jack Nicklaus wins PGA Championship at Firestone
17.text: On August 17, 1975, Jack Nicklaus won the PGA Championship at Firestone by two strokes for the fourth of his record-tying five PGA titles.

17.title: Bob Tway completes PGA win at Inverness
17.text: On August 17, 1986, Bob Tway finished off his PGA Championship victory at Inverness, capped by his famous holed bunker shot on the 72nd hole.

17.title: Shaun Micheel wins PGA with famous 7-iron at Oak Hill
17.text: On August 17, 2003, Shaun Micheel hit a 7-iron to inches on the 72nd hole at Oak Hill to win the PGA Championship, the only PGA Tour title of his career.

17.title: Rory McIlroy wins PGA Championship at Valhalla
17.text: On August 17, 2014, Rory McIlroy won the PGA Championship at Valhalla by one over Phil Mickelson for his second PGA title and fourth major.

18.title: Open champion Cameron Smith born
18.text: Australian Cameron Smith, the 2022 Open Championship and 2022 Players Championship winner renowned for his elite putting, was born on August 18, 1993, in Brisbane.

18.title: Julius Boros remains golf's oldest major champion
18.text: On July 21, 1968 Julius Boros won the PGA Championship at age 48; through August he stood as the oldest major champion in golf history.

18.title: John Daly's PGA legend takes hold
18.text: In the days after his shock 1991 PGA Championship win at Crooked Stick, John Daly became one of golf's biggest draws with his 'grip it and rip it' power.

18.title: Mark Brooks wins PGA Championship at Valhalla
18.text: On August 18, 1996, Mark Brooks beat Kenny Perry in a playoff to win the PGA Championship at Valhalla, the only major title of his career.

18.title: Rich Beem holds off Tiger to win PGA at Hazeltine
18.text: On August 18, 2002, Rich Beem held off a final-round charge by Tiger Woods to win the PGA Championship at Hazeltine by one for his only major.

18.title: Jason Dufner's calm major at Oak Hill
18.text: On August 11, 2013 Jason Dufner won the PGA Championship at Oak Hill with a near-flawless final round for his first major; he soon became a fan favorite.

19.title: David Graham wins PGA Championship at Oakland Hills
19.text: On August 6, 1979 David Graham beat Ben Crenshaw in a playoff at Oakland Hills; the Australian later became the 1981 U.S. Open champion.

19.title: Wayne Grady's PGA triumph at Shoal Creek
19.text: In August 1990 Wayne Grady captured the PGA Championship at Shoal Creek, the high point of the Australian's professional career.

19.title: David Toms wins PGA Championship at Atlanta
19.text: On August 19, 2001, David Toms laid up on the 72nd hole and made par to win the PGA Championship at Atlanta Athletic Club, edging Phil Mickelson by one.

19.title: Tiger Woods wins PGA Championship at Southern Hills
19.text: On August 12, 2007 Tiger Woods won the PGA Championship at Southern Hills by two over Woody Austin for his fourth PGA title and 13th major.

19.title: Brooks Koepka completes major double
19.text: After winning the PGA Championship at Bellerive in August 2018, Brooks Koepka had captured three of his last six majors, cementing his status as the era's top closer.

20.title: Gary Player wins PGA Championship at Oakland Hills
20.text: In August 1972 Gary Player won the PGA Championship at Oakland Hills, hitting a famous blind 9-iron over a willow tree on the par-4 16th en route to victory.

20.title: John Mahaffey wins PGA in playoff at Oakmont
20.text: On August 20, 1978, John Mahaffey rallied from seven back to beat Jerry Pate and Tom Watson in a sudden-death playoff to win the PGA Championship at Oakmont.

20.title: Tiger Woods beats Bob May at Valhalla
20.text: On August 20, 2000, Tiger Woods birdied the 72nd hole and won a three-hole playoff over Bob May to win the PGA Championship at Valhalla, his second straight.

20.title: Tiger Woods wins PGA Championship at Medinah
20.text: On August 20, 2006, Tiger Woods won the PGA Championship at Medinah by five strokes, his third major in a comeback summer following his father's death.

20.title: Steve Elkington's playoff PGA at Riviera
20.text: On August 13, 1995 Steve Elkington beat Colin Montgomerie in a playoff at Riviera with a long birdie putt to win the PGA Championship, his only major.

21.title: Lanny Wadkins wins PGA in first sudden-death playoff
21.text: On August 21, 1977, Lanny Wadkins beat Gene Littler in the first sudden-death playoff in a major championship to win the PGA at Pebble Beach.

21.title: Hal Sutton holds off Nicklaus at Riviera
21.text: On August 21, 1983, Hal Sutton withstood a Jack Nicklaus charge to win the PGA Championship at Riviera by one, the only major of his career.

21.title: Phil Mickelson completes Baltusrol PGA win
21.text: On August 21-22, 2005, Phil Mickelson finished a Monday-completed PGA Championship at Baltusrol, flopping a chip to two feet on the 72nd hole for his first PGA title.

21.title: Keegan Bradley's debut major at Atlanta
21.text: On August 14, 2011 Keegan Bradley won the PGA Championship in a playoff over Jason Dufner in his very first major appearance.

21.title: Nick Price's two-major summer
21.text: By late August 1994 Nick Price had won both the Open Championship and the PGA Championship, confirming his place as the world's best player.

22.title: Raymond Floyd wins PGA wire-to-wire at Southern Hills
22.text: On August 8, 1982 Raymond Floyd led wire-to-wire to win the PGA Championship at Southern Hills, the third of his four major titles.

22.title: Paul Azinger's career-defining major
22.text: In August 1993 Paul Azinger beat Greg Norman in a playoff at Inverness to win the PGA Championship, his only major before a cancer diagnosis later that year.

22.title: Tiger Woods rises after Medinah triumph
22.text: After winning the PGA Championship at Medinah in August 1999, Tiger Woods embarked on one of the most dominant stretches in golf history.

22.title: Vijay Singh's run to World No. 1
22.text: After winning the PGA Championship at Whistling Straits in August 2004, Vijay Singh authored a season of nine wins that took him to World No. 1.

22.title: Dustin Johnson's bunker controversy aftermath
22.text: In the days after the 2010 PGA at Whistling Straits, Dustin Johnson's two-stroke bunker penalty that cost him a playoff spot dominated golf debate.

23.title: Five-time Open champion Peter Thomson born
23.text: Australian Peter Thomson, who won the Open Championship five times between 1954 and 1965, was born on August 23, 1929, in Melbourne.

23.title: Bob Rosburg's lone major in Minneapolis
23.text: In the summer of 1959 Bob Rosburg won the PGA Championship in Minneapolis, the only major title of a long PGA Tour career.

23.title: Larry Nelson's major breakthrough
23.text: In August 1981 Larry Nelson won his first PGA Championship at Atlanta Athletic Club, a turning point in a Hall of Fame career.

23.title: Vijay Singh's first major at Sahalee
23.text: In August 1998 Vijay Singh won the PGA Championship at Sahalee, the first of three major titles for the tireless Fijian.

23.title: Y.E. Yang's historic win resonates
23.text: After his August 2009 PGA win over Tiger Woods at Hazeltine, Y.E. Yang inspired a generation of Asian golfers as the first Asian-born men's major champion.

24.title: PGA champion Rich Beem born
24.text: American Rich Beem, who held off Tiger Woods to win the 2002 PGA Championship at Hazeltine, was born on August 24, 1970, in Phoenix, Arizona.

24.title: Jack Nicklaus collects fourth PGA title
24.text: In August 1975 Jack Nicklaus won the PGA Championship at Firestone, continuing his march toward a record-tying five PGA titles.

24.title: Bob Tway's bunker shot enters golf lore
24.text: In the days after his August 1986 PGA win, Bob Tway's holed bunker shot at Inverness was already hailed as one of golf's greatest clutch shots.

24.title: Shaun Micheel's improbable major
24.text: In August 2003 Shaun Micheel, a journeyman without a prior PGA Tour win, captured the PGA Championship at Oak Hill with a near-perfect closing 7-iron.

24.title: Rory McIlroy completes a dominant summer
24.text: After winning the PGA Championship at Valhalla in August 2014, Rory McIlroy had won the Open and PGA back to back to cap a four-win summer.

25.title: Lee Trevino's final major at Shoal Creek
25.text: In August 1984 Lee Trevino won the PGA Championship at Shoal Creek by four strokes, the sixth and final major of his Hall of Fame career.

25.title: Payne Stewart's Kemper Lakes major
25.text: In August 1989 Payne Stewart rallied past Mike Reid to win the PGA Championship at Kemper Lakes for the first major of his career.

25.title: Mark Brooks edges Kenny Perry at Valhalla
25.text: In August 1996 Mark Brooks beat Kenny Perry in a playoff to win the PGA Championship at Valhalla, the lone major of his career.

25.title: Tiger Woods wins PGA at Southern Hills
25.text: In August 2007 Tiger Woods won the PGA Championship at Southern Hills by two over Woody Austin for his fourth PGA title and 13th major.

25.title: Jason Dufner's first major at Oak Hill
25.text: In August 2013 Jason Dufner won the PGA Championship at Oak Hill with a steady final round for his first and only major title.

26.title: Dave Stockton's second PGA title
26.text: In August 1976 Dave Stockton won the PGA Championship at Congressional, the second of his two PGA Championship victories.

26.title: Nick Price's two-major summer peaks
26.text: By late August 1994 Nick Price had won both the Open and the PGA, confirming his place atop the world game.

26.title: Rory McIlroy's record PGA romp
26.text: In August 2012 Rory McIlroy won the PGA Championship at Kiawah Island by a record eight strokes, signaling his arrival as a generational talent.

26.title: Jason Day's record PGA at Whistling Straits
26.text: In August 2015 Jason Day won the PGA Championship at Whistling Straits at 20-under, the lowest score relative to par in major championship history.

26.title: Brooks Koepka's three-of-six major run
26.text: After winning the PGA Championship at Bellerive in August 2018, Brooks Koepka had captured three of his previous six majors entered.

27.title: Two-time Masters champion Bernhard Langer born
27.text: German Bernhard Langer, a two-time Masters champion and the first official World No. 1, was born on August 27, 1957. He became the most prolific winner in Champions Tour history.

27.title: Gary Player's willow-tree shot wins PGA
27.text: In August 1972 Gary Player won the PGA Championship at Oakland Hills with a famous blind 9-iron over a willow tree on the par-4 16th.

27.title: Larry Nelson's first major
27.text: In August 1981 Larry Nelson won the PGA Championship at Atlanta Athletic Club, the first of three major titles for the former Vietnam veteran.

27.title: Tiger Woods edges teenage Sergio Garcia
27.text: In August 1999 Tiger Woods held off a 19-year-old Sergio Garcia at Medinah to win the PGA Championship, a duel that launched a fierce rivalry.

27.title: Justin Thomas's breakthrough major
27.text: In August 2017 Justin Thomas won the PGA Championship at Quail Hollow for his first major, the highlight of a season that earned him FedEx Cup honors.

28.title: Two-time U.S. Open champion Lee Janzen born
28.text: American Lee Janzen, who won the U.S. Open in 1993 and 1998, was born on August 28, 1964, in Austin, Minnesota.

28.title: Raymond Floyd's first major at NCR
28.text: In August 1969 Raymond Floyd won the PGA Championship at NCR Country Club, launching a career that would yield four major titles.

28.title: Hubert Green's second major at Cherry Hills
28.text: In August 1985 Hubert Green won the PGA Championship at Cherry Hills, his second major title after the 1977 U.S. Open.

28.title: Phil Mickelson's first PGA at Baltusrol
28.text: In August 2005 Phil Mickelson won his first PGA Championship at Baltusrol, flopping a chip to two feet on the final hole for a one-shot victory.

28.title: Keegan Bradley's playoff PGA debut
28.text: In August 2011 Keegan Bradley won the PGA Championship at Atlanta Athletic Club in a playoff over Jason Dufner, a major in his very first appearance.

29.title: John Mahaffey's playoff PGA at Oakmont
29.text: In August 1978 John Mahaffey came from seven back to win the PGA Championship at Oakmont in a playoff, the lone major of his career.

29.title: Paul Azinger outlasts Greg Norman
29.text: In August 1993 Paul Azinger beat Greg Norman in a playoff at Inverness to win the PGA Championship, his only major before a cancer diagnosis.

29.title: Padraig Harrington's two-major summer
29.text: By late August 2008 Padraig Harrington had won both the Open and the PGA, the first European since 1930 to win two majors in a single season.

29.title: Rory McIlroy's Valhalla triumph caps summer
29.text: In August 2014 Rory McIlroy won the PGA Championship at Valhalla by one over Phil Mickelson, his fourth major before turning 26.

29.title: Tiger Woods's Valhalla duel with Bob May
29.text: In August 2000 Tiger Woods beat Bob May in a three-hole playoff at Valhalla in one of the great major duels, his fifth major in a historic season.

30.title: Dave Stockton's PGA at Southern Hills
30.text: In August 1970 Dave Stockton won the PGA Championship at Southern Hills for the first of his two PGA titles.

30.title: Jack Nicklaus's record-tying fifth PGA
30.text: In August 1980 Jack Nicklaus won the PGA Championship at Oak Hill by seven strokes, his fifth PGA title in the same year he won the U.S. Open.

30.title: Rich Beem's stunning major over Tiger
30.text: In August 2002 Rich Beem held off Tiger Woods to win the PGA Championship at Hazeltine, one of the most surprising major victories of the era.

30.title: Tiger Woods's Medinah romp
30.text: In August 2006 Tiger Woods won the PGA Championship at Medinah by five, his third major in the summer following his father's death.

30.title: Rory McIlroy's record Kiawah margin
30.text: In August 2012 Rory McIlroy won the PGA Championship at Kiawah Island by eight strokes, the largest winning margin in the event's history.

31.title: Three-time major champion Padraig Harrington born
31.text: Ireland's Padraig Harrington, winner of the 2007 and 2008 Open Championships and the 2008 PGA Championship, was born on August 31, 1971, in Dublin.

31.title: Masters champion Charl Schwartzel born
31.text: South African Charl Schwartzel, who closed with four straight birdies to win the 2011 Masters, was born on August 31, 1984, in Johannesburg.

31.title: Payne Stewart's Kemper Lakes major
31.text: In August 1989 Payne Stewart rallied past Mike Reid to win the PGA Championship at Kemper Lakes for the first major of his career.

31.title: Mark Brooks's Valhalla playoff win
31.text: In August 1996 Mark Brooks beat Kenny Perry in a playoff to win the PGA Championship at Valhalla, the only major title of his career.

31.title: Jason Day's record-setting PGA
31.text: In August 2015 Jason Day won the PGA Championship at Whistling Straits at 20-under, the lowest score relative to par in major championship history.
